items.title: RT Neel Chhabra: True market dominance requires controlling the critical dependencies... the inputs, the processes, the distribution, and ideally the ...
items.description_html:
RT Neel Chhabra
True market dominance requires controlling the critical dependencies... the inputs, the processes, the distribution, and ideally the customer relationship.

Carnegie controlled raw materials and transportation. Standard Oil controlled refining and distribution. AT&T controlled the entire network. IBM locked in both hardware and software. Microsoft owned the OS. Amazon built the logistics infrastructure. Apple controls chips, OS, apps, and retail. Tesla manufactures batteries, software, and handles direct sales.

The losers in each era? They rented someone else's infrastructure. They trusted partners. They optimized for margins instead of control. They played nice with existing systems instead of building new ones.

The pattern is unmistakable. Across 150 years and myriad of industries, the winners aren’t those with incrementally better products. The winners are those who understand that true market power comes from controlling what others need to reach customers.

It’s not just about vertical integration for efficiency’s sake, it’s about vertical integration as a strategic weapon. When you own the stack, you set the rules. You control the costs. You dictate the pace of innovation. You capture disproportionate value.

This is the timeless physics of market power. The names change. The industries evolve. But the fundamental insight remains: if you want to build an enduring franchise, don’t just compete on product. Compete on structure. Own the stack. Control the chokepoints. Make your business essential to the functioning of others.

That’s how monopolies are born. That’s how fortunes are made. And that’s the pattern that will define the next wave of industrial dominance, whether it’s in semiconductors, space infrastructure, or the picks-and-shovels of the AI revolution.

While averages align in long run, shorter frames show sharp variation.


Ideas for India: Has informal sector output been overstated by extrapolating using formal sector growth rates?
Using now available high-frequency enterprise+labour force data @KartikeyaBatra @attrishu @Santanu_CEGIS compare alternative methodologies; suggest way forward

items.title: RT Urmi Bhattacharya: India's challenge is no longer just getting women into work — it's ensuring they work in roles that match their talents. Gender...
items.description_html:
RT Urmi Bhattacharya
India's challenge is no longer just getting women into work — it's ensuring they work in roles that match their talents. Gender-based distortions cost India 5–15% of aggregate output. Inclusion Economics India Centre's Charity Troyer Moore & Aishwarya Lakshmi Ratan on what to do


Ideas for India: Despite ⬆️ in India’s female labour force participation, women’s labour is misallocated across economy, affecting aggregate output. On #IWD2026 Lakshmi Ratan & Troyer Moore draw on recent evidence & suggest 3 ways to unleash women’s economic potential

https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/beyond-participation-tackling-the-misallocation-of-womens-talent-in-india

items.title: Government announces a ₹10/litre cut in excise duty on petrol & diesel ⛽ Impact: 1. Relief for OMCs OMCs were facing steep losses: ~₹27/ltr on petr...
items.description_html:
Government announces a ₹10/litre cut in excise duty on petrol & diesel ⛽

Impact:

1. Relief for OMCs
OMCs were facing steep losses: ~₹27/ltr on petrol & ₹50/ltr on diesel as of Mar 24
Post cut, losses may ease to ~₹17/₹40 per litre, with blended loss ~₹33/ltr.
Source: ICICI Securities

2. Economic Impact
₹1/litre duty cut = ~₹18,000 cr revenue hit
₹10 cut ⇒ ~₹1.8 lakh cr / ~0.45% of GDP annualized
However, if limited to 3–6 months, fiscal impact may stay contained at ~0.1–0.25% of GDP.
Source: Nirmal Bang

Summed up: Short-term relief for OMCs, but with fiscal trade-offs. Rupee making fresh lows.

@CNBCTV18Live @CNBCTV18News

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: What if everything you’ve been told about ‘buy low, sell high’… is not always true? We’re all taught th...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
What if everything you’ve been told about ‘buy low, sell high’… is not always true?

We’re all taught the same thing. When markets crash and stocks look cheap, you buy. When prices go up and things look expensive, you sell. Simple. Logical. Almost obvious.

But in certain industries, that logic completely flips.

You might actually have to buy when things look expensive, and sell when they look cheap.

Sounds counterintuitive, right? That’s exactly where most investors get it wrong.

@deepakshenoy breaks this down beautifully, along with sharp insights on how to actually look at the market, and more importantly, how to spot the red flags that most people miss.

Catch the full episode on @mundhebanni with subtitles in English
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IMlMUIZ5VTg

items.title: RT Latha Venkatesh: Reactions to the RBI cap on open positions in the onshore forex mkt -Bankers shocked by this sudden cap imposition -Say total posi...
items.description_html:
RT Latha Venkatesh
Reactions to the RBI cap on open positions in the onshore forex mkt
-Bankers shocked by this sudden cap imposition
-Say total positons that need to be wound down could be $40 bn, probabaly more if one counts options
-If difference between $-rupee rate in NDF mkt & onshore mkt on Monday rises to say Re 1, mark-to-mkt losses for banks would be $40bnxRe1= Rs4000cr.
-This Rs4,000 cr is between foreign, pvt & PSU banks
-Being year-end this loss may have to be booked in FY26 P&L
-Bankers are hoping RBI will tweak rules a little by tomorrow morning:
-Very optimistic ask: RBI grandfathers current open positions, says rule applies to fresh positions only
-Moderately optimistic hope: RBI gives 3 months time to bring down open positions to $100mln
-Less optimistic hope: RBI extends time to bring down open positions to April 30, i.e. 1 month
-Pessimistic position: RBI doesn't budge


Latha Venkatesh: Impt announcement from RBI on Friday evening that can strengthen rupee:
-RBI limits NOP-INR positions of banks in the onshore market to $100 mln
-Alert: NOP-INR means net open dollar-rupee positions of banks
-RBI directs banks to bring down their open positions to $100 mln by

RT ＮａｖｅｅｎＫｕｍａ₹ 📈💰
ಸೊನ್ನೆಯಿಂದ ಶುರು ಮಾಡಿ ₹2,000 ಕೋಟಿಯವರೆಗೆ!

>1998 ರಲ್ಲಿ ಬೆಂಗಳೂರಿನ ಮಲ್ಲೇಶ್ವರಂನಿಂದ ಶುರುವಾದ ಪಯಣ. ಕೈಯಲ್ಲಿ ಹಣವಿಲ್ಲ, ಅನುಭವವೂ ಇಲ್ಲ. ಆದರೆ ಇಂದು ₹2,000 ಕೋಟಿಗೂ ಅಧಿಕ ಆಸ್ತಿ ನಿರ್ವಹಿಸುವ 'Capitalmind' ಸಂಸ್ಥೆಯ ಸ್ಥಾಪಕ ದೀಪಕ್ ಶೆಣೈ
ಅವರ ಕಥೆ ಪ್ರತಿಯೊಬ್ಬ ಕನ್ನಡಿಗನಿಗೂ ಸ್ಫೂರ್ತಿ.

>"ನನ್ನನ್ನು ನಾನೇ ಫೈರ್ ಮಾಡಿಕೊಂಡೆ!"
ಸುಮಾರು 7 ವರ್ಷ ಒಂದು ಕಂಪನಿಯನ್ನು ಕಟ್ಟಿ ಬೆಳೆಸಿದರು. ಆದರೆ ಕಂಪನಿ ಬೆಳೀತಿಲ್ಲ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ನಾನೇ ಅಡ್ಡಿಯಾಗುತ್ತಿದ್ದೇನೆ ಎಂದು ಅರಿವಾದಾಗ, ಮದುವೆಯಾದ ಕೇವಲ 3 ತಿಂಗಳಲ್ಲೇ CEO ಸ್ಥಾನಕ್ಕೆ ರಾಜೀನಾಮೆ ನೀಡಿ ಹೊರಬಂದರು.

> ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್‌ಗೆ 'ಎಕ್ಸ್‌ಪೈರಿ ಡೇಟ್' ಬೇಕಾ?
ಸೋಲಿನಿಂದ ಕಲಿತ ಪಾಠವೆಂದರೆ ಡೆಡ್‌ಲೈನ್. "ಇನ್ನೂ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ನೋಡೋಣ" ಎನ್ನುವ ಗೊಂದಲ ಬಿಟ್ಟು, 2008 ರಲ್ಲಿ ಹೊಸ ಪ್ರಯತ್ನಕ್ಕೆ ಇಳಿದಾಗ ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟ ಗಡುವು ಇಟ್ಟುಕೊಂಡರು. ಆ ದೃಢ ನಿರ್ಧಾರದೊಂದಿಗೆ ಹೆಂಡತಿ ಮತ್ತು ಲಗೇಜ್ ಸಮೇತ ಮುಂಬೈಗೆ ಹೊರಟರು.

> ಲೆಹ್ಮನ್ ಕ್ರಾಶ್ ಮತ್ತು ಅಗ್ನಿಪರೀಕ್ಷೆ-
ಅವರು ಮುಂಬೈ ತಲುಪಿದಾಗ ಸಂಭವಿಸಿದ್ದೇ 2008ರ ಜಾಗತಿಕ ಆರ್ಥಿಕ ಕುಸಿತ. ಕಟ್ಟಿದ ಕನಸುಗಳೆಲ್ಲ ಕಣ್ಮುಂದೆಯೇ ಕುಸಿಯುತ್ತಿದ್ದವು. ಆದರೆ ಸಿಸ್ಟಮ್ ಮೇಲೆ ನಂಬಿಕೆಯಿಟ್ಟು, ಶಿಸ್ತಿನಿಂದ ಹೂಡಿಕೆ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದರಿಂದಲೇ ಇಂದು ಅವರು ಈ ಎತ್ತರಕ್ಕೆ ಬೆಳೆದಿದ್ದಾರೆ. 📈

> Gems of Karnataka -
ಉದ್ಯಮ ಜಗತ್ತಿನಲ್ಲಿ ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗಿ ಬೇರೆ ರಾಜ್ಯದವರೇ ಕಾಣಸಿಗುತ್ತಾರೆ. ಆದರೆ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ದೀಪಕ್ ಶೆಣೈ ಇಂದು ದೇಶದ ಪ್ರತಿಷ್ಠಿತ ಇನ್ವೆಸ್ಟರ್‌ಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಒಬ್ಬರು.

> ಹೂಡಿಕೆಯ ಮಂತ್ರಗಳು-
ಈ ಎಪಿಸೋಡ್‌ನಲ್ಲಿ ದೀಪಕ್ ಶೆಣೈ ಅವರು ಹಂಚಿಕೊಂಡಿರುವ ಕೆಲವು ಕಟು ಸತ್ಯಗಳು.
🔹 ಹೂಡಿಕೆ ಎಂಬುದು ಬೋರಿಂಗ್ ಆಗಿರಬೇಕು (Investing should be boring).
🔹 ಸ್ಟಾಕ್ ಮಾರುಕಟ್ಟೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಹೆಚ್ಚಿನವರು ತಿಳಿಯದೆಯೇ ಜೂಜಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾರೆ!
🔹 ಸ್ಟಾಕ್‌ಗಳಲ್ಲಿ 'ರೆಡ್ ಫ್ಲ್ಯಾಗ್' ಗುರುತಿಸುವುದು ಹೇಗೆ?

> ಸ್ಟಾರ್ಟ್‌ಅಪ್‌ಗಳಿಗೆ 1000 ದಿನಗಳ Rule-
ನೀವೇನಾದರೂ ಹೊಸ ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್ ಮಾಡ್ತಿದ್ದರೆ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಒಂದು ಎಕ್ಸ್‌ಪೈರಿ ಡೇಟ್ ಇರಬೇಕಾ? ಅಥವಾ ಲಾಭ ಬರಲು ಎಷ್ಟು ದಿನ ಕಾಯಬೇಕು? ಈ '1000-day Marwadi rule' ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಅದ್ಭುತವಾಗಿ ವಿವರಿಸಿದ್ದಾರೆ. 🚀

ಅವರ ಪುಸ್ತಕ 'Money Wise' ಪ್ರತಿಯೊಬ್ಬ ಹೂಡಿಕೆದಾರ ಓದಬೇಕಾದ ಪುಸ್ತಕ. ಸಂಕೀರ್ಣವಾದ ಮ್ಯಾಕ್ರೋ ಎಕನಾಮಿಕ್ಸ್ ವಿಷಯಗಳನ್ನು ಜನಸಾಮಾನ್ಯರಿಗೂ ಅರ್ಥವಾಗುವಂತೆ ಬಿಡಿಸಿ ಹೇಳುವ ಅವರ ಶೈಲಿ ಅಮೋಘ.

ಕನ್ನಡಿಗರು ಕಟ್ಟಿದ ಸಂಸ್ಥೆಗಳು ಜಾಗತಿಕ ಮಟ್ಟದಲ್ಲಿ ಬೆಳಗಲಿ. 🌟

ಹಣಕಾಸು ಮತ್ತು ಉದ್ಯಮದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ನಿಮ್ಮ ದೃಷ್ಟಿಕೋನವನ್ನೇ ಬದಲಿಸಬಲ್ಲ ಈ 'Raw & Unfiltered' ಸಂಭಾಷಣೆಯನ್ನು @mundhebanni ಪಾಡ್‌ಕಾಸ್ಟ್‌ನಲ್ಲಿ ಈಗಲೇ ನೋಡಿ.

ಇದರಲ್ಲಿ ದೀಪಕ್ ಶೆಣೈ ಅವರ ವೈಯಕ್ತಿಕ ಹೋರಾಟ, ಕನ್ನಡಿಗರ ಸಾಧನೆಯ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆ ಮತ್ತು ಹೂಡಿಕೆಯ ಉಪಯುಕ್ತ ಮಾಹಿತಿಗಳೆಲ್ಲವೂ ಅಡಕವಾಗಿವೆ.
https://youtu.be/IMlMUIZ5VTg?si=1qPUQYEP541AgWv8

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: ನೀವೇನಾದರೂ ಒಂದು ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್ ಮಾಡ್ತಾ ಇದ್ದರೆ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಒಂದು ಎಕ್ಸ್ಪೈ...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
ನೀವೇನಾದರೂ ಒಂದು ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್ ಮಾಡ್ತಾ ಇದ್ದರೆ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಒಂದು ಎಕ್ಸ್ಪೈರಿ ಡೇಟ್ ಇರಬೇಕಾ?

@deepakshenoy ಅವರು ಒಂದು ಕಂಪನಿ ಕಟ್ಟಿದಾಗ, ಸುಮಾರು 7 ವರ್ಷ ಆ ಕಂಪನಿ ರನ್ ಮಾಡಿದರು. ಯಾವುದೇ ಫಂಡಿಂಗ್ ಇರಲಿಲ್ಲ. ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್‌ನಿಂದ ಏನು ಸಂಪಾದನೆ ಆಗುತ್ತಿತ್ತೋ ಅದನ್ನೇ ಮತ್ತೆ ಕಂಪನಿಗೆ ಹೂಡಿಕೆ ಮಾಡ್ತಾ ಬಂದರು. ಆದರೆ ಒಂದು ಹಂತಕ್ಕೆ ಬಂದಾಗ ಅವರಿಗೆ ಒಂದು ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟತೆ ಸಿಕ್ಕಿತು. ಕಂಪನಿ ಬೆಳೀತಾ ಇಲ್ಲ. ಅದಕ್ಕಿಂತ ಮುಖ್ಯವಾಗಿ, ಆ ಕಂಪನಿ ಬೆಳೆಯದಿರೋದಕ್ಕೆ ಕಾರಣ ಅವರೇ ಅನ್ನೋ ಅರಿವು ಅವರಿಗೆ ಬಂದಿತು.

ಸ್ಥಾಪಕರಾಗಿ, ಸಿಇಓ ಆಗಿ, ತಾವು ತೆಗೆದುಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತಿರುವ ನಿರ್ಧಾರಗಳೇ ಕಂಪನಿಗೆ ಅಡ್ಡಿಯಾಗ್ತಿವೆ ಅನ್ನೋ ಮಟ್ಟಕ್ಕೆ ಅವರು introspection ಮಾಡಿದರು. ಹಾಗಾಗಿ ಉದ್ಯಮದ ಇತಿಹಾಸದಲ್ಲಿ ಅಪರೂಪವಾಗಿ ಕಾಣೋ ಒಂದು ನಿರ್ಧಾರ ತೆಗೆದುಕೊಂಡರು. ತಾವು ತಾವೇ ತಮ್ಮನ್ನ ಫೈರ್ ಮಾಡಿಕೊಂಡರು!

ಅದು ಕೂಡ ಮದುವೆ ಆಗಿ ಕೇವಲ 3 ತಿಂಗಳು ಆಗಿದ್ದ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ. ಮುಂದೆ ಏನು ಮಾಡಬೇಕು ಅನ್ನೋ ಕ್ಲಾರಿಟಿ ಇಲ್ಲದೇ, ಆ ಕಂಪನಿಯಿಂದ ಹೊರಬಂದರು.

ಆ ಅನುಭವ ಅವರಿಗೆ ಒಂದು ದೊಡ್ಡ ಪಾಠ ಕಲಿಸಿತು. ಮುಂದೆ ಯಾವ ಹೊಸ ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್ ಶುರು ಮಾಡಿದರೂ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಒಂದು ಎಕ್ಸ್ಪೈರಿ ಡೇಟ್ ಇರಬೇಕು ಅನ್ನೋದು. ಹಾಗಾಗಿ ಅವರು 2008ರಲ್ಲಿ ಹೊಸ ಪ್ರಯತ್ನ ಶುರು ಮಾಡಿದಾಗ, ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟವಾಗಿ ಒಂದು ಡೆಡ್‌ಲೈನ್ ಇಟ್ಟುಕೊಂಡರು. 2008ರ ಕೊನೆಯೊಳಗೆ ಈ ಬಿಸಿನೆಸ್ ರೆವೆನ್ಯೂ ತರುವಂತೆ ಆಗಬೇಕು, ಕಸ್ಟಮರ್ಸ್ ಬರಬೇಕು, ಅಥವಾ ಯಾರಾದರೂ ಅಕ್ವೈರ್ ಮಾಡಬೇಕು. ಇಲ್ಲ ಅಂದ್ರೆ ಕ್ಲೋಸ್ ಮಾಡಿ ಮುಂದೆ ಹೋಗಬೇಕು.

“ಇನ್ನೂ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ನೋಡೋಣ”, “ಇನ್ನೂ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಟ್ರೈ ಮಾಡೋಣ” ಅನ್ನೋ ಗೊಂದಲಕ್ಕೆ ಜಾಗವೇ ಇರಬಾರದು ಅನ್ನೋ ಕ್ಲಾರಿಟಿ.

ಈ ಮನಸ್ಥಿತಿಯ ಜೊತೆ ಅವರು ತಮ್ಮ ಹೆಂಡತಿ ಮತ್ತು ಲಗೇಜ್‌ನ್ನು ಒಂದು ಕಾರಿನಲ್ಲಿ ಹಾಕಿಕೊಂಡು ಮುಂಬೈಗೆ ಹೊರಟರು. ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಹೊಸ ಜೀವನ ಶುರು ಮಾಡೋಕೆ.

ಆದರೆ ಅದೇ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ 2008 ಲೆಹ್ಮನ್ ಬ್ರದರ್ಸ್ ಕುಸಿತ ಸಂಭವಿಸಿತು. ಜಗತ್ತಿನ ಸ್ಟಾಕ್ ಮಾರ್ಕೆಟ್‌ಗಳು ಭಾರೀ ಮಟ್ಟದಲ್ಲಿ ಕುಸಿದವು. ಒಂದು ದೊಡ್ಡ ರಿಸೆಷನ್ ಆರಂಭವಾಯಿತು.

ಅವರು ಕಟ್ಟಿಕೊಳ್ಳಬೇಕೆಂದಿದ್ದ ಜಗತ್ತೇ ಅವರ ಮುಂದೆ ಕುಸಿಯುತ್ತಿತ್ತು. ಅವರ ಜೀವನ ಮತ್ತೊಮ್ಮೆ ಕಠಿಣ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಗೆ ಒಳಪಟ್ಟಂತಾಯಿತು.

ಆ ಎಲ್ಲಾ ಪರಿಸ್ಥಿತಿಗಳನ್ನು ದಾಟಿ, ಇವತ್ತು ಅವರು ಕ್ಯಾಪಿಟಲ್ ಮೈಂಡ್ ಮೂಲಕ ಸುಮಾರು ₹2000 ಕೋಟಿಗೂ ಹೆಚ್ಚು ಆಸ್ತಿಯನ್ನು ನಿರ್ವಹಿಸುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾರೆ.

ಇದು ಕೇವಲ ಇನ್ವೆಸ್ಟಿಂಗ್ ಕಥೆ ಅಲ್ಲ. ಇದು ಅಗ್ನಿ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಗಳನ್ನು ದಾಟಿ ಬಂದ ಒಂದು ಪ್ರಯಾಣದ ಕಥೆ.

ತುಂಬಾ ಇಂಟರೆಸ್ಟಿಂಗ್ ಆಗಿರುವ ಈ ಎಪಿಸೋಡ್ ಈಗ ಮುಂದೆ ಬನ್ನಿ ಚಾನೆಲ್‌ನಲ್ಲಿ ಲಭ್ಯ.
Full episode: 👇
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IMlMUIZ5VTg

@mundhebanni

items.title: RT Anoop: YTD performance over the years. 12 of 20 years were negative as of end of March. 2026 currently 3rd worst.
items.description_html:
RT Anoop
YTD performance over the years. 12 of 20 years were negative as of end of March.

2026 currently 3rd worst.


Anoop: 2026 YTD: 26% of all trading sessions have seen a >1% decline in the Nifty 500.

Second only to 2008. Ahead of 2011, 2009, and the Covid crash year.

Of course, we're only a quarter of the way through the year. Feels like much longer though.

🔔 Speaker Announcement | CareEdge Ratings Webinar 🔔

CareEdge Ratings is pleased to announce the speakers for its upcoming webinar on “Global Headwinds Intensify – Can India Inc Stay the Course?”, focusing on the Credit Quality Assessment for H2FY26.

📅 April 2, 2026
⏰ 12:00 PM – 1:15 PM IST

As global economic uncertainties intensify, driven by geopolitical tensions, evolving trade dynamics, and volatile interest rate trajectories, this webinar will assess how Indian corporates are positioned to navigate the changing operating environment and what it means for credit profiles going forward.

🎙️ Distinguished Speakers:

▪ Mr. Amarjyoti Barua – President & Group Chief Financial Officer, @MahindraRise

▪ Mr. Gaurav Chandna – Joint CEO, Vertis Infrastructure Trust

▪ Mr. Niren Shah – Managing Director and Head, @NorwestVP India

🎤 Moderator:

Sachin Gupta – CRO & Executive Director, CareEdge Ratings

📊 Presenters:

@RajaniSinha2025 – Chief Economist, CareEdge Ratings

Smita Rajpurkar – Senior Director, CareEdge Ratings

📝 Key Topics of Discussion:

• Current economic landscape and emerging global headwinds
• Credit quality assessment of corporate India for H2FY26
• Key sectoral trends, upgrades and downgrades
• Insights into balance sheet resilience and future outlook

CareEdge Ratings is hosting a webinar on “Global Headwinds Intensify – Can India Inc Stay the Course?”

📅 Thursday, April 2, 2026
⏰ 12:00 PM – 1:15 PM IST

The global macroeconomic environment has become increasingly complex, marked by heightened geopolitical risks, evolving trade dynamics, persistent financial market volatility, and an uncertain interest rate trajectory. These developments are creating stronger external headwinds and reshaping the operating landscape for corporates across sectors.

India Inc has so far demonstrated notable resilience, supported by steady domestic demand and relatively healthy balance sheets. However, as global risks intensify, critical questions emerge: How prepared is India Inc to navigate this uncertainty? What does it mean for corporate balance sheets, credit profiles, and sectoral trends going forward?

Key Takeaways

🔹 The current economic landscape
🔹 Credit quality trends within CareEdge Ratings’ portfolio
🔹 Sectoral outlook, including key upgrades and downgrades
🔹 Expert perspectives on navigating the evolving risk environment

items.title: RT Gregg Carlstrom: We're at a moment where each party to the Gulf war has an argument for why it should continue fighting—and where each one may end...
items.description_html:
RT Gregg Carlstrom
We're at a moment where each party to the Gulf war has an argument for why it should continue fighting—and where each one may end up overreaching.

1) Iran misses its moment of maximal leverage to end the war on favorable terms. Instead of trading Hormuz for a deal that includes sanctions relief, it decides to keep inflicting pain on the global economy, hoping to deter future attack or secure a better deal.

Instead its control of the strait becomes a wasting asset: it embarrasses Trump at home and further hardens the positions of his Gulf allies, both of which make it harder for him to end the war. America and Israel ramp up strikes on Iranian infrastructure, with devastating long-term consequences.

2) America launches a ground operation, which (predictably) proves neither a knockout blow to Iran nor a victory image for Trump.

It winds up having to hold chunks of Iranian territory. Casualties compel Trump to send even more troops. Far from ending the war, an invasion sucks America further in. Meanwhile the energy shock gets much, much worse.

3) Israel encourages America to keep going, and winds up rupturing its relationship with America. The war is already unpopular with Americans; more of them think it will benefit Israel (55%) than their own country (30%), according to our most recent poll.

Imagine that it drags on. American troops are killed in combat. Inflation soars. A wounded Trump administration blames Netanyahu for leading it into a quagmire. The midterms become a referendum on America's relationship with Israel. None of this is entirely far-fetched; indeed, the blame game has already begun. https://www.economist.com/middle-east-and-africa/2026/03/29/all-sides-in-the-gulf-war-are-at-risk-of-overplaying-their-hands

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:
In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY.

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.description_html:
BREAKING: Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain are "privately urging" President Trump to continue the war against Iran, per the Washington Post.

Details include:

1. Gulf countries argue Iran has "not been weakened enough" according to US, Gulf, and Israeli officials

2. Gulf countries were initially upset that they were not given adequate advance notice ahead of the war

3. They also reportedly do not want the war to end until there are significant changes in the Iranian leadership or a dramatic shift in Iranian behavior

4. Saudi Arabia and the UAE are "leading" the calls for increasing military pressure on Iran

Day 32 of the Iran War has arrived.

items.title: BREAKING: President Trump is willing to end the Iran War even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, per WSJ. Details include: 1. Trump and his aides...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: President Trump is willing to end the Iran War even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, per WSJ.

Details include:

1. Trump and his aides assessed that a mission to reopen Hormuz would push the conflict beyond his timeline 4-6 weeks

2. Trump believes the US should achieve its main goals of destroying Iran’s navy and missile stockpiles

3. Trump thinks he can wind down current hostilities while pressuring Iran diplomatically to resume the "free flow of trade"

4. If that fails, Washington would press allies in Europe and the Gulf to take the lead on reopening the Strait, US officials say

US stock market futures are rising on the news.

items.title: Investor risk appetite is plunging: The ratio of leveraged long to short ETF trading volume is down to ~1.1, the lowest since the April 2025 “Liberat...
items.description_html:
Investor risk appetite is plunging:

The ratio of leveraged long to short ETF trading volume is down to ~1.1, the lowest since the April 2025 “Liberation Day” bottom.

This means trading activity in leveraged short ETFs is now nearly equal to that of leveraged long ETFs.

By comparison, the indicator reached 3.0 in October, when bullish bets heavily outweighed bearish ones.

This ratio is now approaching the 2022 bear market and 2020 pandemic lows, when investors were heavily positioned for further declines.

By comparison, this ratio fell to 0.4 at the 2008 Financial Crisis bottom, meaning short ETF trading volume exceeded long ETF volume by ~150%.

Is the fear trade becoming too crowded?

items.title: UPDATE: Iran’s "reverse indicator" trading advice continues to play out in real-time: At 4:12 PM ET on Sunday, Iran's Speaker of the Parliament said ...
items.description_html:
UPDATE: Iran’s "reverse indicator" trading advice continues to play out in real-time:

At 4:12 PM ET on Sunday, Iran's Speaker of the Parliament said pre-market news is a "reverse indicator," if they "dump" the market, then "go long," and "if they pump it, short it."

By 10:00 PM ET last night, S&P 500 futures had fallen nearly -1% on mounting war concerns.

By 12:00 AM ET, 2 hours later, S&P 500 futures had reversed all losses and turned green.

Then, at 7:25 AM ET today, President Trump posted that "great progress" is being made on peace talks sending the S&P 500 +100 points off of its low.

Now, at 3:00 PM ET, just ~7 hours later, the S&P 500 has turned red and is down -100 points from its high, once again "reversing" price action.

That's a near $2 trillion swing in S&P 500 market cap in less than 24 hours.

There is truly never a dull moment in this market.

items.title: BREAKING: China is now on track to import 3.69 million metric tons of liquefied natural gas in March, the lowest since 2018. That would mark a -45% de...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: China is now on track to import 3.69 million metric tons of liquefied natural gas in March, the lowest since 2018.

That would mark a -45% decline from January and a -57% drop from December, amid the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Last year, China imported ~30% of its LNG from Qatar.

As a result, Asian LNG prices have nearly DOUBLED this month as buyers compete for an increasingly limited supply.

Chinese companies are now relying more on domestic gas and pipeline deliveries to offset the shortage.

China’s LNG imports are collapsing amid the Iran War.

items.title: Systematic selling of US equities may be running out of steam: Commodity Trading Advisors (CTAs), the algorithm-driven funds that buy and sell based o...
items.description_html:
Systematic selling of US equities may be running out of steam:

Commodity Trading Advisors (CTAs), the algorithm-driven funds that buy and sell based on price trends, have sold -$85 billion in US equities over the last 30 trading sessions.

This marks the largest 30-day sale since the 2020 pandemic, when CTAs sold -$105 billion.

By comparison, the March-April 2025 correction saw -$80 billion in sales.

As a result, CTAs are now short -$37 billion in US equities, the 3rd-highest amount since 2019, behind the April 2025 low and November 2023.

Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs estimates that CTAs are set to buy in every market scenario over the next month.

The market is setting up for a relief rally.

items.title: Energy stocks are making history: The US Energy Select Sector Index has gained for 14 consecutive weeks, the longest streak on record. The index has r...
items.description_html:
Energy stocks are making history:

The US Energy Select Sector Index has gained for 14 consecutive weeks, the longest streak on record.

The index has rallied +43% over that stretch, fueled by the Iran War and investor rotation out of software and AI stocks earlier this year.

Over the same period, investors have poured a massive +$5.5 billion into the Energy Sector ETF, $XLE.

To put this into perspective, the previous record was 10 weeks, set in 1998.

Energy stocks have also reached 25 all-time highs year-to-date, the most in a single year since the 41 records set in 2007.

Energy stock prices are soaring.

items.title: Hedge funds are moving to the sidelines at a near record pace: Hedge fund net leverage dropped -3.1 points last week, the biggest weekly decline since...
items.description_html:
Hedge funds are moving to the sidelines at a near record pace:

Hedge fund net leverage dropped -3.1 points last week, the biggest weekly decline since the first week of April 2025 amid "Liberation Day".

This is the 5th largest reduction in leverage over the last 5 years, comparable to the 2022 bear market.

Furthermore, hedge funds posted their 6th consecutive weekly sale of US equities, the biggest 6-week sale since April 2025.

Since 2016, the only two larger 6-week sales were during the 2020 pandemic and in March-April 2025.

As a result, hedge fund net positioning is down to near the most bearish level since 2018.

Sentiment is becoming overly bearish.

items.title: You can't make this up: Iran's trading advice to US investors actually worked. At 4:12 PM ET on Sunday, Iran's Speaker of the Parliament said pre-mark...
items.description_html:
You can't make this up:

Iran's trading advice to US investors actually worked.

At 4:12 PM ET on Sunday, Iran's Speaker of the Parliament said pre-market news is a "reverse indicator" and if they "dump" the market, then "go long."

At 6:00 PM ET, S&P 500 futures opened nearly -1% lower and fell just 30 points away from correction territory.

By 11:00 PM ET, S&P 500 futures had reversed all losses and turned green.

Then, at 7:25 AM ET today, President Trump posted that "great progress" is being made on Iran peace talks.

Now, the S&P 500 is trading +100 points above its low seen just hours ago, adding +$900 billion in market cap.

We are in the most unusual times in market history.

items.title: RT Latha Venkatesh: Reactions to the RBI cap on open positions in the onshore forex mkt -Bankers shocked by this sudden cap imposition -Say total posi...
items.description_html:
RT Latha Venkatesh
Reactions to the RBI cap on open positions in the onshore forex mkt
-Bankers shocked by this sudden cap imposition
-Say total positons that need to be wound down could be $40 bn, probabaly more if one counts options
-If difference between $-rupee rate in NDF mkt & onshore mkt on Monday rises to say Re 1, mark-to-mkt losses for banks would be $40bnxRe1= Rs4000cr.
-This Rs4,000 cr is between foreign, pvt & PSU banks
-Being year-end this loss may have to be booked in FY26 P&L
-Bankers are hoping RBI will tweak rules a little by tomorrow morning:
-Very optimistic ask: RBI grandfathers current open positions, says rule applies to fresh positions only
-Moderately optimistic hope: RBI gives 3 months time to bring down open positions to $100mln
-Less optimistic hope: RBI extends time to bring down open positions to April 30, i.e. 1 month
-Pessimistic position: RBI doesn't budge

-Alert: NOP-INR means net open dollar-rupee positions of banks

Big News !

Govt Cuts 👇
Excise duty on Petrol to Rs.3 from Rs.13/Litre
Excise Duty On Diesel to zero From `10/Litre

Basically oil marketing companies were losing money on the the marketing side because cost of oil shooting up & Rupee depreciating. All this while retail prices remain unchanged.

Govt has stepped in & said that we will collect less on excise - so you (OMCs) bleed less. As MK Surana formerly of HPCL pointed out that this should be viewed as a help on the liquidity side as well.

Positive for oil marketing companies, yes ! But we will have to see the quantum of losses & how much does this help. But help it does !

items.title: Closing FY2026 - 9 Months ago I took the leap of faith and went back into startup mode and Here’s how it’s going - Got myself SEBI Registered in 202...
items.description_html:
Closing FY2026 - 9 Months ago I took the leap of faith and went back into startup mode and Here’s how it’s going

- Got myself SEBI Registered in 2025
- Grew Socials from an average of 320k subs to almost 450k in 9months on Insta and YT
- Distribution across platforms grew to 1Mn+
- Started with a team of 6, Today we are at 18 (3 remote)
- Launched Smallcases on October 2nd 2025, Beat our yearly target of 1,000 subs in 1 month, revised it to 3,000 subs for October 2026, we are 80% there.
- Crossed 26 Cr in AUM, aiming to cross 50 Cr soon
- Launched 7BAR - Swing Trading Cohort, Crossed 3,300 Members in 9 months, our fastest growing vertical
- Both 7BAR and Smallcase operate at 80% Margins today, lean team, Good Value add
- Did 3 Offline events in Bangalore, Mumbai and Jaipur. Next is Hyderabad and Chennai - We don't make money on this as its a part of our Investor education initiative

Big Bets and Cash Burners
- Building a Fintech app in Stealth, Will be launching in the next 3-4months - Bootstrapped
- Will be along the lines of Stock Advisory and My distribution to start with
- Aim to get 7,000 Paid Users in Year 1 without Ad Spend (low estimates)
- Invested in 3 Shadow Channels on Social Media to support the App
- Team size should go up to 25 and our current office would sadly max out.

Investments
- Last year I Invested/ Incubated a Studio Company called Studio Ionique, goal was to grow my channel, service other customers and build many IP channels to help our products
- As of year closing (9months), The Biz closed at 1.06cr topline
- We have built 9 IP channels out of which 3 have crossed 50k subs (without my face) rest in incubation phase
- Social Media Services wing - We are currently working with 5 customers only, generating an average of 17L a month (3.5L average)
- Client breakup - 1 AI, 1 SAAS, 1 Global, 1 Youtube Biz, 1 Market Participant
- Year 2 should, at minimum close at 2.7cr topline for Studio

What's next
- We will be applying for PMS/AIF in 2027
- Converting from Individual RA to Corporate RA
- Due to having only an RA license, We are rejecting many customers who want us to manage their money/PF today
- Will have 1 more big launch this year by December 2026 that should generate 1cr+ revenue in 365days of launch with 50%+ margins
- Got in 2 co-founders, 1 who manages the entire Research side and the other who will build the tech stack

What I Need to Improve?
- Need to focus more on My Socials, kinda let it fall while building the biz from scratch. Realized the importance of having a co-founder haha
- Earlier we were a lean team, now we are scaling a bit too fast, hence have to start improving the hiring process.
- Earlier, we hired everyone on the metric of revenue realization - Now I need to hire people who won’t directly contribute to increasing revenue but more towards branding and comms
- Started focusing on health, Got inspired by my peers mainly @VaibhavSisinty , bro got ripped while juggling 100 things and he’s just 100ft away from my office. So no excuses for me now
- Mental Health kept going for a toss - Started up at the same time I became a dad. So There is a constant Guilt that I’m not spending a lot of time with my son during this stage, will fix that

Overall Both Businesses combined should be hitting 5cr+ topline next year while being very profitable, Full Steam Ahead🙌 Goal is to build a very good wealth management firm. Baby steps

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Jefferies on Financials In a surprise move, RBI has capped banks' net open position (NOP) in onshore forex market to $100m vs. board-d...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Jefferies on Financials
In a surprise move, RBI has capped banks' net open position (NOP) in onshore forex market to $100m vs. board-driven limit within 25% of capital.
This may be driven by sharp depreciation of INR & wider spread between offshore (NDF) & onshore mkt.
While this may support INR, unwinding of positions (by 10Apr) may lead to MTM losses in 4Q as positions may be large at $30-40bn with large banks & select foreign banks leading.
Sector has sought leniency from RBI.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: JPM on HDFC BK Upgrade to OW from Neutral, TP Rs 1010 BKs valuation declining to lowest P/B (on 12M forward rolling basis) since merge...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
JPM on HDFC BK
Upgrade to OW from Neutral, TP Rs 1010
BKs valuation declining to lowest P/B (on 12M forward rolling basis) since merger announcement (Apr’22), at a 16Y low of 1.5x P/B FY28E (parent), following the 24% YTD share price correction.
HDFCB has significantly underperformed peers (ICICIBC -8% YTD) as well as broader market (NIFTY -13% YTD) & now trades at a 2Y high 17% discount vs ICICIBC on 12M forward P/BV, making the risk-reward attractive
Upgrade is premised on
(i) a recovery in system credit growth;
(ii) positive RoA inflection as higher-cost borrowings are replaced by low-cost deposits; and
(iii) a strong AQR track record and liability franchise, which positions HDFCB well in the current uncertain macro environment as investors shift to large-cap quality names
While a constrained deposit environment could weigh on sentiment & earnings (FY26-28E JPMe are 2-3% below Bloomberg consensus), believe that recent valuation de-rating factors in the likely earnings risk

Jefferies on HDFC BK
Buy, TP Rs
STK down 24% YTD, UPFing peers, due to concerns around exit of Chairman & impact of West Asia conflict.
Now, vals at 1.6x FY27E adj PB, 13x PE are at disc to IBank & low premium to peers.
Vals are attractive given stronger asset quality, healthy growth & ROE.
Sensitivity to higher credit cost & lower topline is manageable.
Clarity on board-issues & rollover of CEOterm/ Chairman appointment can aid rerating
HDFC BK is among sector top picks.

@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: Don't anchor yourself to one price that if the stock comes here I will look at it , be dynamic and agile to see the facts as they are irrespective of ...
items.description_html:
Don't anchor yourself to one price that if the stock comes here I will look at it , be dynamic and agile to see the facts as they are irrespective of the price , even if the stock corrects to what you like , see the objectivity that can it get worse ,is the reason for the fall what you thought or is it more deep rooted of a problem , having an anchor price in mind wont let you see the facts objectively

Be an opportunist investor , not one that gives in, even if you thought and waited for the stock to come to a certain price , be willing to walk away also if it is not attractive enough , change your opinion when the facts change

Market does not reward your ego or anchor , it rewards disciplined execution at the right valuations and growth adjusted to it , no one variable matters , all of them do hence the need to be agile and not anchored

items.title: APL Apollo: Expanding Addressable Market via Solar & PEB "large PEB companies, they do take a lot of hollow steel sections, a lot of structural steel ...
items.description_html:
APL Apollo: Expanding Addressable Market via Solar & PEB

"large PEB companies, they do take a lot of hollow steel sections, a lot of structural steel tubes from APL Apollo. And our market share is 60%-70% in this segment." - APL Apollo Concall

A structural growth driver is emerging.

🔆 Solar Opportunity
• India solar capacity >100GW; target ~280GW by 2030
• ~30GW annual additions expected over medium term
• Estimated ~830,000 tons market opportunity by 2030 for APL Apollo products
• Strong play in tubes for rooftop, ground-mounted & tracker structures

🏗 PEB (Pre-Engineered Buildings)
• Rapid industrial expansion driving hollow section demand
• APL Apollo holds 60–70% market share in this segment
• Management expects 20–25% growth for next 3–4 years in PEB-related demand

Solar + PEB together significantly expand the structural steel tubes TAM — positioning APL Apollo for sustained volume growth.

#APLApollo #Solar #PEB #Infrastructure #Steel #GrowthStory

items.title: Viviana Power Tech is gradually moving beyond pure EPC into a mix of execution and annuity-based visibility. Order book stands at ~₹1,400 Cr. FY26 re...
items.description_html:
Viviana Power Tech is gradually moving beyond pure EPC into a mix of execution and annuity-based visibility.

Order book stands at ~₹1,400 Cr.
FY26 revenue guidance is ₹560 Cr (vs ₹219 Cr last year).
Management is guiding 40–50% CAGR over the next 2–3 years.

The company has entered BESS with ~₹500 Cr worth of secured projects (100 MW Rajasthan, 65 MW Gujarat).
~₹300 Cr capex, funded 80% debt and 20% equity.
12-year structure with ~₹40 Cr potential annual revenue.
Return expectation: 9–10%.

EBITDA margins are around 19–20%.
PAT margins guided at 9–10%.

ROE at 33%+ and ROCE ~35%.

Going ahead:
• Targeting ₹500–600 Cr additional T&D orders by Mar’26 / Q1 FY27
• Increasing participation in BESS and solar hybrid tenders
• Mainboard migration possible by Mar/Apr 2026

Execution remains strong, order visibility is healthy, and the annuity layer is slowly getting built.

Still early, but the direction is clearly changing.

A reminder that stock prices & business performance can move very differently in the short term

Lots of lessons for long term investors - must read⏬

items.title: RT Manan S. Bhatt: "Ambushed" #UntoldStoryArmy During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was canc...
items.description_html:
RT Manan S. Bhatt
"Ambushed"
#UntoldStoryArmy

During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was cancelled, and everyone returned to their units. However, I received special orders to stay put and await further instructions. Two days later, my posting to Jammu and Kashmir arrived – with immediate reporting required.

Having driven my car to Mhow, I had no choice but to take it back to Punjab, leave it there, and then travel on to J&K. Pressed for time, I decided to drive straight from Mhow to Delhi, a risky decision. One particularly dangerous stretch was between Gwalior and Agra, notorious for bandits and lawlessness.

Traveling alone at night through this area was foolish, but I pressed on in my trusty Hindustan-14. Around midnight, approaching a narrow pass through low hills, I found the road blocked by a fallen tree. It was an ambush. Turning back wasn't an option.

As I stopped, armed men materialized, their faces hidden. They searched me for valuables, taking my watch and wallet before moving on to my car and luggage. However, things took an unexpected turn when someone announced a "Sardar" had arrived.

Every one pulled back a little and there appeared a sturdy young man with a large mustache. As he drew near, he suddenly sprung to attention and gave me a smart salute and said ‘Sahib Ji, tussee ethay ki kar rahey ho.’ (Sir what are you doing here!)

From his voice and his face, latter day mustache notwithstanding, I recognized him. He was my tank gunner and had been discharged after his seven-year tenure of engagement. Those days soldiers were discharged after seven years and given no pension.

I responded to his query and asked him,’ Nahar Singh toon aye ki kam pharya hai (Nahar Singh what is this work you have taken on.) He said, ‘Sahib Ji admi nu kuch na kuch taan karna chaheda hai, welay beth kaay vi ki karna hai.’ (Sir one must do, some or the other work and there is no point in sitting idle!)

I told him, ‘tenu aasi eh taan nahi sakhlaya’ (we did not train you for such activity.) “Sahib ji tohati ambush laney di sikhlai kam ahh rahi hai. Sadi ambush wich koe nuks hai!.” (Sir your training in laying an ambush is proving useful. Do you find any fault with our ambush.)

He responded with a loud chuckle. He addressed his gang and said. “Dekheya sadhi regiment de officer kiney deelair hun, ekelay he rat nu is sarak tay chal rahey hun.” (do you see how brave are the officers of our regiment. They travel alone on this road at night.)

I asked him if his wife knew what work he was doing. To this he answered “Sarian gallaan wife nu thorian dassidian hun.

Oh soch di hai kay main police da kam kar rahaan hain.” (You don’t disclose everything to your wife. She thinks I am working for the police). At this one of them interjected that, “Police whi ehhoo kam kardi hai.” (Police too does this sort of work).

This raised much laughter amongst them. Then he told his men not to just stand watching but to quickly make tea for the sahib. All my stuff was put back in the car, and my watch and wallet returned. After a cup of tea and much bonhomie and handshaking I took leave of Nahar Singh and his gang.

A story by Lt Gen Harwant Singh

items.title: RT Rush Doshi: On Wednesday, I testified before the House Small Business Committee on China. Bottom Line: We obsess over China's tech giants. But we m...
items.description_html:
RT Rush Doshi
On Wednesday, I testified before the House Small Business Committee on China.

Bottom Line: We obsess over China's tech giants. But we miss the small firms behind its manufacturing ecosystem. China built a program to make them world-beaters, and ours now face existential risk.

Five points:

1️⃣ China's "Little Giants" (小巨人) program is one of its most consequential industrial policy programs — but few know much about it . A decade ago, Beijing made clear small businesses were critical to winning the fourth industrial revolution and important parts of the Made in China 2025 plan. So it built a system for them. It certified the most promising high-tech firms as "Little Giants," then handed them loans, subsidies, state equity investment, university research partnerships, fast-tracked patents, guaranteed contracts from state-owned enterprises, and streamlined stock listings — all in one coordinated package. Today, more than 17,000 Chinese firms hold that designation. 90% are in high-tech manufacturing. Together they raised $125 billion in private capital in just a few years. One of them is Unitree Robotics, now a global titan.

2️⃣ Our efforts to help small manufacturers through the Small Business Administration (SBA) simply do not compare to China's "Little Giants" program. China funds early-stage research through state institutes — we let our equivalent programs, SBIR and STTR, lapse. China packages loans, equity, and R&D into a single coordinated certification — we run countless uncoordinated initiatives with no common thread. China's little giants raised $125+ billion in private capital with implicit state backing — the first cohort of our SBIC Critical Technologies Initiative might raise $4 billion. China provides low-cost loans at scale — we haven't raised the loan caps or appropriations for our own manufacturer credit program. China deploys technical assistance to thousands of firms through universities and state institutes — we just cut the Manufacturing Extension Partnership and effectively shuttered its key offices. At every level, there is a "gap" between our approach and theirs.

3️⃣ Small businesses matter because they are the path to American reindustrialization. Large firms dominate U.S. manufacturing and have for decades. But small businesses enable them. 70% of Boeing's Dreamliner comes from smaller suppliers. 60% of all aerospace and defense employment is in small and mid-sized firms. The story is similar in automotives. We cannot win the industrial future if we do not empower our small businesses.

4️⃣ China is far ahead in manufacturing, and expanding the lead. By some estimates, China spends roughly $400 billion on industrial policy per year. The entire US CHIPS Act provided $50 billion over multiple years. Since China's WTO accession, our share of global manufacturing has fallen by half — from 30% to 15% — while China's quintupled from 6% to 30%. It now exceeds the next nine countries combined. It's not exactly slowing down.

5⃣ Here's what we should do. Immediately reauthorize SBIR and STTR. Launch an American one-stop-shop certification that bundles loans, equity, R&D support, and regulatory relief into one coordinated package. Scale up the SBIC Critical Technologies Initiative. Raise the quantity and caps for the SBA's Manufacturer's Access to Revolving Credit program. Restore the Manufacturing Extension Partnership. And give the SBA the mandate to deploy all of these together — the way China does. This is just a start, and a comprehensive answer to China's programs will require even more.

The SBA has plenty of tools. What it lacks is the architecture to coordinate the use them. China built that architecture. It is working.

Thanks to @HouseSmallBiz for the opportunity and grateful to join Andrew Pahutski, Sean Murphy, and Tom Lyons for the hearing.

items.title: RT Richard Turrin: Your ID on the Blockchain Can Fight Tinder Fakes The biggest question remains: who should issue the ID? The technology used can be ...
items.description_html:
RT Richard Turrin
Your ID on the Blockchain Can Fight Tinder Fakes

The biggest question remains: who should issue the ID? The technology used can be debated after determining who controls the ID.

Should it be a form of state-issued Digital Public Infrastructure, as with India's Aadhaar, or should it pass to Web3 companies who believe in identity kept out of reach of the state?

While the debate between the state and Web3 remains lively, what most agree on is that this isn't a job for Google or Apple as much as they would like to have it.

Note that in the US, IRS electronic filing depends on "ID .me" a big tech solution paving the way for Google and Apple to take over.

Perhaps the best solution yet is the EU's EUDI wallet, which, while state-backed, supports decentralized identity (DID) standards, allowing users to own and manage their identity data rather than relying on central authorities like Google or Facebook.

While Web3 wants to be free of the state, it's hard to see such a DID system interacting with state-sponsored tax and social benefits.

Kudos to @andymukherjee70

#fintech #tech #finserv #AI

items.title: The question, economically, is whether protecting consumers completely is the wisest choice. If price pass through is artificially stalled => demand d...
items.description_html:
The question, economically, is whether protecting consumers completely is the wisest choice.

If price pass through is artificially stalled => demand doesn’t adjust => current account deficit widens => rupee pressure persists => feeds to further capital outflows/ feeds to inflation / feeds to pressure on interest rates.

So while protecting consumers is importance, gotta judge how far that’s prudent (if crisis lasts).

Tough choices.

items.title: Does ethanol blending actually save fossil fuel consumption. India is usually compared to Brazil Sharing the math below and answer is - Not Really Key...
items.description_html:
Does ethanol blending actually save fossil fuel consumption. India is usually compared to Brazil

Sharing the math below and answer is - Not Really

Key Take Away - It makes way more sense in Brazil as their sugar cane is rain fed, while in India we use quite a bit of irrigation

India saves ~27%. Brazil saves ~87%. The difference is almost entirely irrigation — Indian sugarcane is pumped with majorly coal-fired electricity, Brazilian cane is rainfed. India's ethanol program mostly swaps imported crude for domestic coal and gas. Brazil's genuinely displaces fossil fuels.

items.title: RT Exencial Research Partners: India's MRO opportunity $4 billion by 2030, a defence dependency problem, and the infrastructure race to fix both. Indi...
items.description_html:
RT Exencial Research Partners
India's MRO opportunity $4 billion by 2030, a defence dependency problem, and the infrastructure race to fix both.

India has been flying fighter jets it cannot fully maintain on its own soil.

80 to 90 per cent of India's aircraft MRO has historically happened outside India. Engines to France. Avionics to the US. Submarines to Europe for critical system tests.

The cost is financial. The risk is operational. In a conflict, a platform grounded waiting for a foreign repair slot is a capability gap.

That dependency is now being dismantled around a $4 billion market that India is building the capacity to own.

(1/11)

items.title: Two quick takeaways from the platform fee hike by Zomato and Swiggy: - Food delivery continues to be the cash cow, helping offset losses from quick co...
items.description_html:
Two quick takeaways from the platform fee hike by Zomato and Swiggy:

- Food delivery continues to be the cash cow, helping offset losses from quick commerce, especially for Swiggy, which needs to get to breakeven soon. This is also a key advantage that both companies have over Zepto.

- For now, Zomato and Swiggy are not seeing a significant impact on their market share from newer platforms like Rapido’s Ownly and Swish. It will be interesting to see whether these recently well-funded players can leverage this.


Madhav Chanchani: Both Zomato and Swiggy have increased platform fees to Rs 17.58, a jump of Rs 2.59 per order.

Collectively, this will add over Rs 100 crore directly to their bottom line per quarter.

About Rs 60-65 crore for Zomato and Rs 40–45 crore for Swiggy, based on their order volumes.

items.title: Both Zomato and Swiggy have increased platform fees to Rs 17.58, a jump of Rs 2.59 per order. Collectively, this will add over Rs 100 crore directly t...
items.description_html:
Both Zomato and Swiggy have increased platform fees to Rs 17.58, a jump of Rs 2.59 per order.

Collectively, this will add over Rs 100 crore directly to their bottom line per quarter.

About Rs 60-65 crore for Zomato and Rs 40–45 crore for Swiggy, based on their order volumes.

Notably, platform fees did not even exist three years ago.

Now, they already account for 4–5% of the average order value.

items.title: Looks like the new owners will keep the Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) brand, or at least keep the option open. They will enter into a deal with Un...
items.description_html:
Looks like the new owners will keep the Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) brand, or at least keep the option open.

They will enter into a deal with United Spirits to use the brand after the transaction closes.

The RCB team is named after United Spirits’ Royal Challenge whisky brand.

The new owners - Blitzers, Blackstone, Aditya Birla Group, and the Times of India Group - are buying RCB for $1.78 billion from United Spirits.

The deal is expected to close in six months.

items.title: RT Variety: Disney has ended its partnership with OpenAI after Sora is discontinued. The company had plans to take a $1 billion stake in the AI compan...
items.description_html:
RT Variety
Disney has ended its partnership with OpenAI after Sora is discontinued. The company had plans to take a $1 billion stake in the AI company.

A Disney rep said in a statement to Variety: “As the nascent AI field advances rapidly, we respect OpenAI’s decision to exit the video generation business and to shift its priorities elsewhere. We appreciate the constructive collaboration between our teams and what we learned from it, and we will continue to engage with AI platforms to find new ways to meet fans where they are while responsibly embracing new technologies that respect IP and the rights of creators.”

https://variety.com/2026/digital/news/openai-shutting-down-sora-video-platform-1236698277/

items.title: That is a massive valuation bump for the Rajasthan Royals at $1.6 bn. It was valued at just $250–300 mn back in 2021, when RedBird Capital picked up ...
items.description_html:
That is a massive valuation bump for the Rajasthan Royals at $1.6 bn.

It was valued at just $250–300 mn back in 2021, when RedBird Capital picked up a stake.

It also sets a valuation benchmark - over 100x of FY25 PAT of Rs 146 crore and 23x of revenues of Rs 644 crore.


KSR: #RajasthanRoyals

US-based entrepreneur Kal Somani-led consortium has bought 100% of Rajasthan Royals at US$1.63 billion.

He is joined by the Rob Walton (Walmart) and the Hamp (Ford) families in the consortium.

Ownership will come into effect post the 2026 edition of the IPL.

items.title: For context, Chinese firms such as Alibaba Group and Tencent were among the biggest growth-stage backers of internet companies such as Zomato, Paytm, ...
items.description_html:
For context, Chinese firms such as Alibaba Group and Tencent were among the biggest growth-stage backers of internet companies such as Zomato, Paytm, and Swiggy before 2020.

Chinese companies and venture firms were investors in 18 of the 30 Unicorns India had back then. They had invested $4-5 billion in Indian startups between 2017-2020.

It will be interesting to see whether strategics make a comeback or if the capital will mostly come through venture capital funds.


Madhav Chanchani: It’s official: Chinese capital can now return to India’s startup ecosystem.

The government has relaxed the Press Note 3 restrictions implemented in 2020.

Investments of up to a 10% stake will now be allowed under the automatic route.
